hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2 selectivity by forming favorable π-π stacking interactions with ECL2 residues (e.g., Tyr193) unique to SSTR2, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with the SSTR2-specific TM6 pocket (e.g., Ile276), thereby increasing the selectivity margin over SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 selectivity but are failing structurally. The focus should remain on maintaining selectivity while improving structural validity through increased candidate diversity and quality filtering.

proposed_changes.rationale: Low pass rate suggests insufficient diversity in candidate generation. Increasing the number of candidates will improve the chances of generating structurally valid designs.
All candidates have pLDDT=0.0, indicating poor structural confidence. Adding a pLDDT threshold will filter out low-quality structures early in the pipeline.
